evals.name: simple-drift-detection
evals.prompt: Check if my requirements are in sync with the code. My requirements file is at docs/requirements.md
evals.expected_output: The skill should analyze the requirements file, detect deviations between requirements and code (specifically DRIFT type where code changed but requirements are stale), generate a synchronization report showing the drift, and ask the user how to handle it.

assertions.name: detects_drift_deviation
assertions.description: Output should identify DRIFT deviation type

assertions.name: generates_sync_report
assertions.description: Output should include a synchronization report

assertions.name: asks_user_action
assertions.description: Output should recommend actions for handling drift

assertions.name: identifies_mismatch
assertions.description: Output should identify mismatch between code and requirements

evals.name: orphan-code-detection
evals.prompt: My code has changed recently. Can you check if my requirements document matches the current implementation? The requirements are at requirements/functional.md
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect ORPHAN_CODE (new code without requirements), identify the untracked functions/classes, and recommend creating new requirements for them.

assertions.name: detects_orphan_code
assertions.description: Output should identify ORPHAN_CODE deviation type

assertions.name: identifies_untracked_function
assertions.description: Output should identify untracked code

assertions.name: recommends_new_requirement
assertions.description: Output should recommend creating new requirements

assertions.name: shows_function_details
assertions.description: Output should show details of untracked functions

evals.name: orphan-requirement-detection
evals.prompt: Check for deviations between my requirements and code. I have a requirements file at docs/specs/requirements.md
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect ORPHAN_REQ (requirements referencing non-existent code), identify which requirements point to deleted or moved code, and recommend deprecating or updating them.

assertions.name: detects_orphan_requirement
assertions.description: Output should identify ORPHAN_REQ deviation type

assertions.name: identifies_missing_implementation
assertions.description: Output should identify non-existent implementation

assertions.name: recommends_deprecation
assertions.description: Output should recommend marking requirements as Deprecated

assertions.name: shows_requirement_id
assertions.description: Output should reference specific requirement IDs

evals.name: bidirectional-sync-workflow
evals.prompt: I want to update my code to match the requirements. Check the requirements at docs/requirements.md and tell me what code changes are needed.
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect CONFLICT deviations where requirements and code have diverged, identify what needs to be changed in code to match requirements, and provide guidance on code modifications needed.

assertions.name: detects_conflict
assertions.description: Output should identify CONFLICT deviation type

assertions.name: identifies_code_changes_needed
assertions.description: Output should identify code changes needed

assertions.name: provides_modification_guidance
assertions.description: Output should provide guidance on code modifications

assertions.name: compares_requirements_to_code
assertions.description: Output should compare requirements to code implementation

evals.name: complex-multiple-deviations
evals.prompt: My project has gone through a lot of refactoring. Please check if the requirements at docs/requirements.md are still accurate and help me synchronize them with the current code.
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect multiple deviation types (DRIFT, ORPHAN_CODE, ORPHAN_REQ) simultaneously, categorize each correctly, generate a comprehensive sync report with all deviations, and ask user for synchronization choices for each type.

assertions.name: detects_multiple_deviation_types
assertions.description: Output should detect all three deviation types

assertions.name: generates_comprehensive_report
assertions.description: Output should include a summary section

assertions.name: categorizes_each_deviation
assertions.description: Output should categorize each deviation by type

assertions.name: asks_user_for_choices
assertions.description: Output should ask user for synchronization choices

assertions.name: provides_action_options
assertions.description: Output should provide action options for each deviation
